popularStory.headline: Urraque Ière de León (1081-1126)
popularStory.description:
Urraque Ire, née en 1081 et morte le 8 mars 1126) est reine de León et Castille de 1109 à sa mort.

Biographie

Fille du roi Alphonse VI et de Constance de Bourgogne, Urraque devint l'héritière de son …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Reina Urraca de León於1081年6月24日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安時，她的父親Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon41歲，母親Constance de Bourgogne35歲。她於1095年與Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她於1079年住在西班牙加利西亞。她去世於1126年3月8日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞索爾達娜享年44歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯奧納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Reina Urraca de León于1081年6月24日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安时，她的父亲Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon41岁，母亲Constance de Bourgogne35岁。她于1095年与Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她于1079年住在西班牙加利西亞。她去世于1126年3月8日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞索爾達娜享年44岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯奧納。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: León
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

en.lifeSummary: When Reina Urraca de León was born on 24 June 1081, in León, Castilla y León, Spain, her father, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, was 41 and her mother, Constance de Bourgogne, was 35. She married Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia in 1095, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She lived in Galicia, Spain in 1079. She died on 8 March 1126, in Saldaña, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spain, at the age of 44, and was buried in Oña, Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Reina Urraca de León est née le 24 juin 1081, à León, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Constance de Bourgogne, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia en 1095, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle a vécu à Galicia, Espagne en 1079. Elle est décédée le 8 mars 1126, à Saldaña, Palencia, Castilla y León, Espagne, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Oña, Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Reina Urraca de León am 24. Juni 1081 in León, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon 41 und ihre Mutter Constance de Bourgogne 35. Sie hat 1095 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. 1079 hat sie in Galicia, Spanien gelebt. Sie starb am 8. März 1126 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Saldaña, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spanien und wurde in Oña, Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Reina Urraca de León è nata il il 24 giugno 1081 nel León, Castilla y León, Spagna, sua padre, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Constance de Bourgogne, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia nel 1095, nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Galicia, Spagna nel 1079. Morì il 8 marzo 1126, nel Saldaña, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spagna, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolta a Oña, Burgos, Castilla y León, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Reina Urraca de Leónが1081年6月24日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンレoンで生まれた時，父Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leonは41歳，母Constance de Bourgogneは35歳であった。1095年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドでRaymond De Bourgogne Count of Galiciaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1079年にはスペインガリシアに住んでいた。彼女は1126年3月8日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンプアレンシアサルダnアで44歳で死去し，スペインカスティラ イ レoンブルゴスオnアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Reina Urraca de León(이)가 1081년 6월 24일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 레o에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Constance de Bourgogne(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1095년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1079년도에 스페인 갈리샤에서 살았다. 그녀는 1126년 3월 8일 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 파렌샤 살다n아에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 버고스 n아에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Reina Urraca de León nasceu em 24 de junho de 1081, em León, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Constance de Bourgogne, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia em 1095, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela viveu em Galicia, Espanha em 1079. Ela faleceu em 8 de março de 1126, em Saldaña, Palencia, Castilla y León, Espanha, com 44 anos, e foi sepultada em Oña, Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Reina Urraca de León родилась 24 июня 1081 г. в местности леoн, кастилла и леoн, Испания, её отцу по имени Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Constance de Bourgogne, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia в 1095 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она жила в местности галися, Испания в 1079 году. Она умерла 8 марта 1126 г. в местности салдаnа, пеиленся, кастилла и леoн, Испания в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности оnа, бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Reina Urraca de León nació el 24 de junio de 1081, en León, Castilla y León, España, su papá, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Constance de Bourgogne, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Raymond De Bourgogne Count of Galicia en 1095, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Vivió en Galicia, España en 1079. Murió el 8 de marzo de 1126, en Saldaña, Palencia, Castilla y León, España, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultada en Oña, Burgos, Castilla y León, España.
